title: Most traders don’t have a strategy problem — they have a decision problem
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hey everyone,

Something I’ve been thinking about recently:

Most traders spend a lot of time trying to improve their strategy:

* new indicators
* new setups
* new entry techniques

But I’m starting to feel like that’s not really the main issue.

Because even with a solid strategy, people still:

* enter too early
* skip valid setups
* take trades that don’t fully meet their criteria

The strategy stays the same, but the decisions change.

So maybe the real problem isn’t:  
👉 “what should I trade?”

But:  
👉 “how consistently do I follow my own logic?”

That’s where most of the difference seems to come from.

Curious how you see it:

👉 Do you think your results are more limited by your strategy… or your execution?

comments.author: Expensive-Badger-142
comments.body: Second guessing is probably one of the worst feelings/decision deterrents one can have. If you been doing this for a while your gut instincts is usually correct on the first analysis

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Yeah I get what you mean.

Second guessing can mess you up way more than the actual setup sometimes. Like once you start hesitating, you’re not really trading your plan anymore.

That said, I think the tricky part is knowing when it’s real intuition vs when it’s just bias kicking in.

Sometimes that “gut feeling” is experience…  
and sometimes it’s just not wanting to miss the move.

That’s where I used to get caught a lot.

replies.author: Expensive-Badger-142
replies.body: Mhmm great distinction. For new comers how does one overcome this or validate the difference between fomo and real intuition?

comments.author: Slight_Ad_6375
comments.body: The problem is - the second guess is sometimes correct 🥲

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body: That's why I use this tool to help me make decisions, and it's really made a difference

comments.author: Jabba_au
comments.body:
Hard to keep human emotion out of it.

I built the paramaters that I would normally trade on and have an automated bot that executes buys and sells 24/7
